howItWorks.intro: This parallel & perpendicular line calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What makes lines parallel?
faq.answer: Parallel lines have the same slope but different y-intercepts. They never intersect. If one line has slope 3, any parallel line also has slope 3.

faq.question: What makes lines perpendicular?
faq.answer: Perpendicular lines intersect at a 90° angle. Their slopes are negative reciprocals of each other. If one slope is 2, the perpendicular slope is -1/2.

faq.question: What is a negative reciprocal?
faq.answer: To find the negative reciprocal, flip the fraction and change the sign. For example: 3 becomes -1/3, -2/5 becomes 5/2, and 1 becomes -1.
